name: Transfer Learning
summary: Transfer learning is the practice of pre-training a model on a large, general dataset and then fine-tuning it on a smaller, task-specific dataset. The key insight is that features learned during pre-training (language patterns, visual features, etc.) transfer effectively to downstream tasks. In NLP, this paradigm was revolutionized by models like BERT and GPT, which are pre-trained on massive text corpora and fine-tuned for specific applications. Transfer learning dramatically reduced the data and compute needed for individual tasks and became the dominant paradigm in both computer vision (via ImageNet pre-training) and NLP (via language model pre-training).
query_hooks: Why don't we train models from scratch for every task?
